name: bluing
summary: Bluing An intelligence gathering tool for hacking Bluetooth

documentation.readme: Bluing An intelligence gathering tool for hacking Bluetooth
usage_sections: Installation: Bluing partially depend on BlueZ, the official Linux Bluetooth protocol stack. So it only supports running on Linux. The following command is used to install dependencies: Currently, bluing is distributed via PyPI and only supports Python 3.10. The following is an installation co

arguments.name: -bit
arguments.description: UUID: 0x95FE

arguments.name: --clean
arguments.description: Clean cached data of a remote device

arguments.name: --collect-btsnoop-log
arguments.description: Collect the btsnoop log being generated to a local file,

arguments.name: --flash-micro-bit
arguments.description: Download the dedicated firmware to micro:bit(s)

arguments.name: --gatt
arguments.description: Discover GATT Profile hierarchy of a remote LE device

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: Print this help and quit

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: Print this help and quit

arguments.name: --inquiry
arguments.description: Discover other nearby BR/EDR controllers

arguments.name: --inquiry-scan
arguments.description: Enable the Inquiry Scan

arguments.name: --ll-feature-set
arguments.description: Read LL FeatureSet of a remote LE device

arguments.name: --lmp-features
arguments.description: Read LMP features of a remote BR/EDR device

arguments.name: --local
arguments.description: Target a local BR/EDR device instead of a remote one

arguments.name: --mon-incoming-conn
arguments.description: Print incoming connection from other nearby BR/EDR devices

arguments.name: --pairing-feature
arguments.description: Request the pairing feature of a remote LE device

arguments.name: --scan
arguments.description: Discover advertising devices nearby

arguments.name: --sdp
arguments.description: Retrieve information from the SDP database of a

arguments.name: --sniff-adv
arguments.description: Sniff advertising physical channel PDU. Need at least

arguments.name: --sniff-and-guess-bd-addr
arguments.description: Sniff SAPs of BD_ADDRs over the air, then guess the

arguments.name: --stack
arguments.description: Determine the Bluetooth stack type of a remote BR/EDR device

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: Print version information and quit

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print version information and quit

arguments.name: -weight
arguments.description: bold; color: #9fab76">bluing --help

arguments.name: --channel
arguments.description: WiFi channel to operate on (1-165).

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_bluetooth_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Bluetooth category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_bluetooth_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Bluetooth system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for bluing (fO-000/bluing). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for bluing. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

examples: sudo apt install python3-pip python3-dev libcairo2-dev libgirepository1.0-dev \

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with mackowiakd/BLE-Security-PoC (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with SmonSE/GhostBLE (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ukBaz/python-bluezero (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: Bluing An intelligence gathering tool for hacking Bluetooth.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Bluing An intelligence gathering tool for hacking Bluetooth.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo apt install python3-pip python3-dev libcairo2-dev libgirepository1.0-dev \`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo pip3.10 install bluing`; `NAME="Ubuntu"`. Bluing An intelligence gathering tool for hacking Bluetooth.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use bluing because Bluing An intelligence gathering tool for hacking Bluetooth.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo apt install python3-pip python3-dev libcairo2-dev libgirepository1.0-dev \` as documented upstream for bluing.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo pip3.10 install bluing` as documented upstream for bluing.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `NAME="Ubuntu"` as documented upstream for bluing.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `VERSION="16.10 (Yakkety Yak)"` as documented upstream for bluing.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair bluing with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bluing --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: sudo apt install python3-pip python3-dev libcairo2-dev libgirepository1.0-dev \
